title: Components
description: Holds a set of reusable objects for different aspects of the OAS.

__schema1.title: Schema Object
__schema1.description: A Schema Object defines the shape of a JSON value.

__schema2.title: Reference Object
__schema2.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

examples.description: A user is a type of person.
A pet is a type of animal.
A pet is a type of animal.

__schema3.title: Reference
__schema3.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

__schema7.title: summary
__schema7.description: A short summary which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema8.title: description
__schema8.description: A description which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema10.title: String Object
__schema10.description: A String Object defines the shape of a string value.

__schema11.title: title
__schema11.description: A title for the schema, used for documentation.

__schema12.title: description
__schema12.description: A description of the schema. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema13.title: default
__schema13.description: The default value for this schema.

__schema14.title: example
__schema14.description: An example value for this schema.

__schema15.title: enum
__schema15.description: An array of valid values for this schema.

__schema17.title: const
__schema17.description: A constant value that this schema must match.

__schema19.title: Discriminator
__schema19.description: When request bodies or response payloads may be one of a number of different schemas, a discriminator object can be used to aid in serialization, deserialization, and validation.

__schema20.title: propertyName
__schema20.description: The name of the property in the payload that will hold the discriminator value.

__schema21.title: mapping
__schema21.description: An object to hold mappings between payload values and schema names or references.

__schema26.title: XML
__schema26.description: A metadata object that allows for more fine-tuned XML model definitions.

__schema27.title: name
__schema27.description: Replaces the name of the element/attribute used for the described schema property.

__schema28.title: namespace
__schema28.description: The URI of the namespace definition.

__schema29.title: prefix
__schema29.description: The prefix to be used for the name.

__schema30.description: Declares whether the property definition translates to an attribute instead of an element.
__schema30.title: attribute

__schema32.description: May be used only for an array definition. Signifies whether the array is wrapped or not.
__schema32.title: wrapped

__schema36.title: ExternalDocumentation
__schema36.description: Allows referencing an external resource for extended documentation.

__schema37.title: description
__schema37.description: A short description of the target documentation.

__schema38.title: url
__schema38.description: The URL for the target documentation.

__schema40.title: readOnly
__schema40.description: When true, the property is only returned in responses, not accepted in requests.

__schema41.title: writeOnly
__schema41.description: When true, the property is only accepted in requests, not returned in responses.

__schema42.title: deprecated
__schema42.description: When true, indicates this schema is deprecated and should be avoided.

__schema43.title: nullable
__schema43.description: OpenAPI 3.0: Set to true to allow null values.

__schema45.title: format
__schema45.description: Semantic format hint for string values. Common formats: date-time, email, uuid, uri, hostname, ipv4, byte, binary.

__schema46.title: Pattern
__schema46.description: A regular expression pattern the string must match.

__schema52.title: Number Object
__schema52.description: A Number Object defines the shape of a number value.

__schema54.title: format
__schema54.description: Number precision format. Use 'float' for 32-bit single-precision or 'double' (default) for 64-bit double-precision IEEE 754.

__schema55.title: multipleOf
__schema55.description: Require the number to be a multiple of this value.

__schema57.title: minimum
__schema57.description: Inclusive lower bound for the number.

__schema59.title: maximum
__schema59.description: Inclusive upper bound for the number.

__schema61.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema61.description: Exclusive lower bound (instance must be > this value).

__schema63.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema63.description: Exclusive upper bound (instance must be < this value).

__schema65.title: Integer Object
__schema65.description: An Integer Object defines the shape of an integer value.

__schema67.title: format
__schema67.description: Integer size format. Use 'int32' for signed 32-bit (-2B to 2B) or 'int64' for signed 64-bit integers.

__schema68.title: multipleOf
__schema68.description: Require the integer to be a multiple of this value.

__schema70.title: minimum
__schema70.description: Inclusive lower bound for the integer.

__schema72.title: maximum
__schema72.description: Inclusive upper bound for the integer.

__schema74.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema74.description: Exclusive lower bound (instance must be > this value).

__schema76.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema76.description: Exclusive upper bound (instance must be < this value).

__schema78.title: Boolean Object
__schema78.description: A Boolean Object defines the shape of a boolean value.

__schema80.title: Array Object
__schema80.description: An Array Object defines the shape of an array value.

__schema84.title: prefixItems
__schema84.description: Tuple validation: schemas for items by index (array position).

__schema86.title: contains
__schema86.description: Require the array to contain at least one item matching this subschema.

__schema98.title: Object Object
__schema98.description: An Object Object defines the shape of an object value.

__schema122.title: Composition Schema
__schema122.description: A schema using composition keywords (allOf, anyOf, oneOf, not) without a type constraint.

__schema123.title: allOf
__schema123.description: Require the instance to validate against all subschemas.

__schema125.title: anyOf
__schema125.description: Require the instance to validate against at least one subschema.

__schema127.title: oneOf
__schema127.description: Require the instance to validate against exactly one subschema.

__schema129.title: not
__schema129.description: Require the instance to NOT validate against the given subschema.

__schema132.title: Response
__schema132.description: Describes a single response from an API Operation.

__schema133.title: Response
__schema133.description: Describes a single response from an API Operation.

__schema137.title: Header
__schema137.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema138.title: Header
__schema138.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema139.title: description
__schema139.description: A brief description of the parameter.

__schema140.description: Determines whether this parameter is mandatory.
__schema140.title: required

__schema142.description: Specifies that a parameter is deprecated.
__schema142.title: deprecated

__schema144.description: Sets the ability to pass empty-valued parameters.
__schema144.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema146.title: style
__schema146.description: Describes how the parameter value will be serialized.

__schema161.title: externalValue
__schema161.description: A URL that points to the literal example.

__schema169.title: MediaType
__schema169.description: Each Media Type Object provides schema and examples for the media type identified by its key.

__schema177.title: Encoding
__schema177.description: A single encoding definition applied to a single schema property.

__schema178.title: contentType
__schema178.description: The Content-Type for encoding a specific property.

__schema194.title: Link
__schema194.description: The Link object represents a possible design-time link for a response.

__schema195.title: Link
__schema195.description: The Link object represents a possible design-time link for a response.

__schema196.title: operationId
__schema196.description: The name of an existing, resolvable OAS operation, as defined with a unique operationId.

__schema197.title: operationRef
__schema197.description: A relative or absolute URI reference to an OAS operation.

__schema198.title: parameters
__schema198.description: A map representing parameters to pass to an operation as specified with operationId or identified via operationRef.

__schema201.title: requestBody
__schema201.description: A literal value or {expression} to use as a request body when calling the target operation.

__schema205.title: url
__schema205.description: A URL to the target host. Supports server variables in {braces}. May be relative.

__schema206.title: description
__schema206.description: An optional description of the server.

__schema207.title: variables
__schema207.description: A map of server variables for URL template substitution.

__schema209.title: ServerVariable
__schema209.description: A server variable for URL template substitution. Use {variableName} in the server URL.

__schema210.title: enum
__schema210.description: An enumeration of valid string values for this variable.

__schema212.title: default
__schema212.description: The default value for substitution if not supplied.

__schema213.title: description
__schema213.description: An optional description for the server variable.

__schema221.title: name
__schema221.description: The name of the parameter. Case-sensitive.

__schema222.title: in
__schema222.description: Location of the parameter. 'path' parameters must have required=true.

__schema223.title: description
__schema223.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema228.description: For query parameters only. Allows empty values (?param=).
__schema228.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema230.title: style
__schema230.description: Serialization style. Defaults vary by 'in': query/cookie='form', path/header='simple'.

__schema231.title: explode
__schema231.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema232.description: For query parameters. When true, allows reserved characters without encoding.
__schema232.title: allowReserved

__schema236.title: examples
__schema236.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema238.title: content
__schema238.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

additionalProperties.title: SecurityScheme
additionalProperties.description: Defines a security scheme that can be used by the operations.

__schema256.title: name
__schema256.description: The name of the header, query, or cookie parameter.

__schema257.title: in
__schema257.description: Location of the API key: 'header' (most common), 'query', or 'cookie'.

__schema258.title: description
__schema258.description: A description of the security scheme.

__schema260.title: HttpSecurityScheme
__schema260.description: HTTP authentication security scheme (Basic, Bearer, etc.).

__schema263.title: bearerFormat
__schema263.description: Hint for bearer token format. Only applicable when scheme='bearer'.

__schema264.title: description
__schema264.description: A description of the security scheme.

__schema268.title: flows
__schema268.description: Allows configuration of the supported OAuth Flows.

__schema270.title: OAuthFlow
__schema270.description: Configuration details for a supported OAuth Flow.

__schema271.title: authorizationUrl
__schema271.description: The authorization URL to be used for this flow.

__schema272.title: tokenUrl
__schema272.description: The token URL to be used for this flow.

__schema273.title: refreshUrl
__schema273.description: The URL to be used for obtaining refresh tokens.

__schema274.title: scopes
__schema274.description: The available scopes for the OAuth2 security scheme.

__schema287.title: openIdConnectUrl
__schema287.description: OpenID Connect URL to discover OAuth2 configuration values.

__schema293.title: Callback
__schema293.description: A map of possible out-of-band callbacks related to the parent operation.

__schema294.title: Callback
__schema294.description: A map of possible out-of-band callbacks related to the parent operation.

__schema296.title: PathItem
__schema296.description: Describes the operations available on a single path.

__schema303.title: Operation
__schema303.description: Describes a single API operation on a path.

__schema304.title: tags
__schema304.description: A list of tags for API documentation grouping.

__schema306.title: summary
__schema306.description: A short summary of the operation. Keep under ~120 characters.

__schema307.title: description
__schema307.description: A verbose description. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema308.title: operationId
__schema308.description: Unique identifier for the operation. Used for code generation.

__schema309.title: parameters
__schema309.description: Parameters for this operation, combined with path-level parameters.

__schema311.title: responses
__schema311.description: REQUIRED. The responses for this operation.

__schema315.title: deprecated
__schema315.description: Marks this operation as deprecated. Defaults to false.

__schema317.title: security
__schema317.description: Security requirements for this operation. Overrides root-level security.

items.title: SecurityRequirement
items.description: Lists the required security schemes for this operation.
